clusters.title: Validator accepts worker nodes that deploy rejects

member_findings.title: Validator accepts worker nodes that deploy rejects
member_findings.impact: Any non-empty string in worker_nodes is currently treated as valid, so a config such as ["worker-1"] passes validate_config. The existing deploy paths call util.is_valid_ip(node) before starting or stopping workers, which raises for non-IP hostnames, so validate_config can return success for a config that dscli up/down will reject.
member_findings.fix: Validate each worker_nodes entry with the same address contract used by deployment, e.g. call util.is_valid_ip for every node and report invalid addresses, or first change the deploy path to support hostnames consistently.

clusters.title: Test module loader leaves permanent stubs in sys.modules

member_findings.title: Test module loader leaves permanent stubs in sys.modules
member_findings.impact: _load_validate_module() installs fake yr/yr.datasystem CLI modules into the process-wide import cache and never restores the previous state. Future tests added to this file or run in the same Python process can silently import these stubs instead of the real CLI package, making failures hard to reason about and coupling otherwise unrelated tests to this loader.
member_findings.fix: Isolate the stubs with a scoped patch, for example unittest.mock.patch.dict(sys.modules, stubs) around spec.loader.exec_module(), or snapshot and restore the affected sys.modules entries in a finally block after loading the target module.

clusters.title: Real path-safety failure path is untested

member_findings.title: Real path-safety failure path is untested
member_findings.impact: Command.run now depends on util.valid_safe_path rejecting unsafe config paths, but the tests replace that helper with an identity lambda. A regression that stops converting ValueError from the real path guard into FAILURE would still pass the current suite, even though unsafe operator input is one of the new command's file-load failure modes.
member_findings.fix: Add a Command.run test that uses the real cli.common.util.valid_safe_path behavior, or a stub that raises ValueError for an unsafe path, and assert the command returns FAILURE.

member_findings.title: Invalid-config reporting is not asserted
member_findings.impact: The PR's main CLI behavior is to report every structural problem before deploy, but test_run_invalid_config_returns_failure only checks the return code. If this loop were removed or only the first problem were logged, the command would stop giving operators the promised complete error list while all current tests still passed.
member_findings.fix: Capture the command logger for an invalid config with multiple problems and assert that each expected problem is emitted, not just that the return code is FAILURE.

clusters.title: Validator duplicates cluster config field names instead of reusing shared constants

member_findings.title: Validator duplicates cluster config field names instead of reusing shared constants
member_findings.impact: The new validator hard-codes worker_nodes, worker_port, worker_config_path, and ssh_auth credential paths even though cli/common/constant.py already defines these keys as ClusterConfig values used by the deployment commands. If the cluster config schema is renamed or extended later, maintainers must update two independent vocabularies and the validator can drift from the rest of dscli.
member_findings.fix: Reuse ClusterConfig for the existing keys, or add a small shared schema/field helper in cli/common so generate/load/deploy/validate paths reference one source for the cluster config key names.
